vocab.meaning: cất giấu/dự trữ tiền bạc vào nơi an toàn
vocab.example: Many people use high-interest savings accounts to squirrel away a portion of their salary each month.

vocab.meaning: lưới an toàn/khoản dự phòng tài chính
vocab.example: Banks help individuals build a safety net, ensuring they have resources to fall back on during unexpected crises.

vocab.meaning: xoay sở để đủ sống/trang trải chi phí
vocab.example: During tough economic times, people might rely on bank overdrafts just to make ends meet until their next payday.

vocab.meaning: vớ bở/kiếm được khoản lợi nhuận lớn (thông qua đầu tư tại ngân hàng)
vocab.example: By putting money into long-term fixed deposits, some lucky investors feel like they've hit the jackpot when interest rates soar.

vocab.meaning: ném tiền qua cửa sổ/lãng phí tiền bạc (ngược lại với việc gửi ngân hàng)
vocab.example: Without a proper bank account to manage expenses, it is far too easy to pour money down the drain on impulse purchases.

vocab.meaning: đáng đồng tiền bát gạo/có năng lực (nói về cố vấn ngân hàng)
vocab.example: Any financial advisor worth their salt will tell you that keeping money in a regulated bank is essential for wealth protection.

vocab.meaning: giữ cho mình không bị phá sản/vượt qua khó khăn tài chính
vocab.example: Low-interest loans from banks are often the only way for small startups to keep their head above water in the first year.

vocab.meaning: có tiền là có quyền/sức mạnh của tài chính
vocab.example: In the business world, money talks, and having a strong banking relationship is key to securing large-scale contracts.
The primary reason for using merchant bank accounts is to help businesses manage their cash flow and eventually turn a profit.

vocab.meaning: bằng mọi giá (phải bảo vệ tiền)
vocab.example: Security is the main priority; people want to ensure their hard-earned identity and funds are protected at all costs.
